books.title: Body Sounding Alarms: A Compassion Fatigue Book
books.subtitle: For Managers: When your nervous system scans for danger that isn't there
books.desc: You've been bracing for something you can't name. keep caring without losing myself.
books.long_desc:
You've been bracing for something you can't name.

This book is for mid-level manager running a cross-functional teams who I care too much and it's breaking me. It doesn't promise to make the feeling stop. It names what's happening in language you can use the next time it happens.

Chapter by chapter, you'll work with the body's false-alarm system and why it keeps firing. Not through exposure or worksheets or morning routines — through recognition, descent, and integration. The practice the book asks for can happen on a subway, between meetings, or at 3am.

You'll walk away knowing what your body is doing and why it makes sense. Not fixed. Not healed in any final sense. But no longer alone with the pattern.

books.title: Body Sounding Alarms: A Self Worth Book
books.subtitle: For First Responders: When your nervous system scans for danger that isn't there
books.desc: You've been bracing for something you can't name. stop measuring my worth by outcomes.
books.long_desc:
You've been bracing for something you can't name.

This book is for paramedic / firefighter / EMSs who I always end up back at 'I'm not enough'. It doesn't promise to make the feeling stop. It names what's happening in language you can use the next time it happens.

Chapter by chapter, you'll work with the body's false-alarm system and why it keeps firing. Not through exposure or worksheets or morning routines — through recognition, descent, and integration. The practice the book asks for can happen on a subway, between meetings, or at 3am.

You'll walk away knowing what your body is doing and why it makes sense. Not fixed. Not healed in any final sense. But no longer alone with the pattern.

books.subtitle: For the Sandwich Generation: When you measure yourself against the wrong yardstick
books.desc: Everyone else seems fine. You know how that feels. release what's been held in my body for years.
books.long_desc:
Everyone else seems fine. You know how that feels.

This book is for manager + parent + caregiver to aging parents who my body holds things my mind has been trying to forget. It doesn't promise to make the feeling stop. It names what's happening in language you can use the next time it happens.

Chapter by chapter, you'll work with the comparison engine and what it costs your nervous system. Not through exposure or worksheets or morning routines — through recognition, descent, and integration. The practice the book asks for can happen on a subway, between meetings, or at 3am.

You'll walk away knowing what your body is doing and why it makes sense. Not fixed. Not healed in any final sense. But no longer alone with the pattern.
